creator: js@jschneider.com
text:
OK, will take up the issue with encfs devs, but pretty sure this is a FreeBSD specific issue...

I disproved my thought that it might be related to trying to use an older encrypted directory.  Created a brand new one with new .encfs6.xml file and still get similar issues.  I can't create any file or directory.  Here are some messages from /var/log/messages:

Mar  5 17:51:38 mobile-01 encfs[1543]: ERROR encoding 16 bytes, got back 0 (0 in final_ex) [SSL_Cipher.cpp:892]
Mar  5 17:51:38 mobile-01 encfs[1543]: ERROR withFileNode: error caught in getattr: block encode failed in filename encode [encfs.cpp:178]
Mar  5 17:51:42 mobile-01 encfs[1543]: ERROR encoding 16 bytes, got back 0 (0 in final_ex) [SSL_Cipher.cpp:892]
Mar  5 17:51:42 mobile-01 encfs[1543]: ERROR withFileNode: error caught in getattr: block encode failed in filename encode [encfs.cpp:178]
Mar  5 17:51:45 mobile-01 encfs[1543]: ERROR encoding 16 bytes, got back 0 (0 in final_ex) [SSL_Cipher.cpp:892]
Mar  5 17:51:45 mobile-01 encfs[1543]: ERROR withFileNode: error caught in getattr: block encode failed in filename encode [encfs.cpp:178]
Mar  5 17:51:45 mobile-01 encfs[1543]: ERROR encoding 16 bytes, got back 0 (0 in final_ex) [SSL_Cipher.cpp:892]
Mar  5 17:51:45 mobile-01 encfs[1543]: ERROR withFileNode: error caught in getattr: block encode failed in filename encode [encfs.cpp:178]
Mar  5 17:52:06 mobile-01 encfs[1543]: ERROR encoding 16 bytes, got back 0 (0 in final_ex) [SSL_Cipher.cpp:892]
Mar  5 17:52:06 mobile-01 encfs[1543]: ERROR withFileNode: error caught in getattr: block encode failed in filename encode [encfs.cpp:178]
